description: Writing a password, key, or other secret into contract storage, assuming Solidity's private visibility hides it.
body: Failure mode: a contract stores sensitive data (a password, API key, off-chain credential, or random seed) in a private state variable expecting it to be hidden, but anyone can read raw storage slots and inspect the mempool, so the secret is public and permanently recorded. Detection signals: sensitive data assigned to storage; reliance on private/internal visibility for confidentiality; a value meant to be secret appears in calldata or storage. Do not store secrets on-chain. Safer fallback: keep secrets off-chain and store only a hash/commitment if verification is needed, use commit-reveal patterns for fairness, assume the mempool is watched, and treat any already-stored secret as compromised and rotate it.
